sections.title: Necessity for driving licence
sections.content: No person shall drive a motor vehicle in any public place unless he holds an effective driving licence authorising him to drive the vehicle, and no person shall so drive a transport vehicle unless his driving licence specifically entitles him to do so.

sections.title: Age limit in connection with driving of motor vehicles
sections.content: No person under the age of eighteen years shall drive a motor vehicle in any public place. A person who has attained the age of sixteen years may drive a motor cycle with engine capacity not exceeding fifty cubic centimetres. No person under the age of twenty years shall drive a transport vehicle in any public place.

sections.title: Responsibility of owners of motor vehicles
sections.content: No owner or person in charge of a motor vehicle shall cause or permit any person who does not satisfy the provisions of section 3 or section 4 to drive the vehicle.

sections.title: Grant of learner's licence
sections.content: Any person not disqualified under section 4 from holding a driving licence may apply to the licensing authority for the issue of a learner's licence. The applicant must pass the test prescribed in the Rules and satisfy other requirements relating to age and medical fitness.

sections.title: Grant of driving licence
sections.content: Any person who is not for the time being disqualified for holding or obtaining a driving licence may apply for the issue of a driving licence. The applicant shall pass the test of competence to drive prescribed by the licensing authority.

sections.title: Form and contents of licences to drive
sections.content: Every driving licence shall be in such form and contain such particulars as may be prescribed. It shall specify the class or classes of vehicles which the holder is entitled to drive.

sections.title: Currency of licences to drive motor vehicles
sections.content: A learner's licence is effective for six months. A driving licence is effective, except in case of transport vehicles, for twenty years from the date of issue or until the holder attains fifty years, whichever is earlier. For transport vehicles the validity is three years.

sections.title: Power of licensing authority to disqualify from holding a driving licence
sections.content: If a licensing authority is satisfied that the holder of a driving licence is a habitual criminal or drunkard, or has been involved in an offence involving the use of a motor vehicle resulting in serious bodily injury or death, it may disqualify the person from holding a driving licence and revoke the licence.

sections.title: Necessity for registration
sections.content: No person shall drive any motor vehicle and no owner of a motor vehicle shall cause or permit the vehicle to be driven in any public place or in any other place unless the vehicle is registered in accordance with this Chapter and the certificate of registration of the vehicle has not been suspended or cancelled and the vehicle carries a registration mark displayed in the prescribed manner.

sections.title: Registration, how to be made
sections.content: An application for registration shall be made by or on behalf of the owner of the motor vehicle in such form and manner accompanied by such documents and fees as may be prescribed, to the registering authority within whose jurisdiction the owner has the residence or place of business.

sections.title: Transfer of ownership
sections.content: Where the ownership of any motor vehicle registered under this Chapter is transferred, the transferor shall report the transfer to the registering authority within fourteen days, and the transferee shall report the transfer to the registering authority within thirty days. The registering authority shall make the entry of transfer of ownership in the certificate of registration.

sections.title: Certificate of fitness of transport vehicles
sections.content: Subject to the provisions of sections 59 and 60, a transport vehicle shall not be deemed to be validly registered unless it carries a certificate of fitness in the prescribed form, issued by the prescribed authority.

sections.title: Necessity for permits
sections.content: No owner of a motor vehicle shall use or permit the use of the vehicle as a transport vehicle in any public place, whether or not such vehicle is actually carrying passengers or goods, save in accordance with the conditions of a permit granted or countersigned by a Regional or State Transport Authority.

sections.title: Necessity for permit
sections.content: Save as otherwise provided, no person shall use, or permit the use of, a transport vehicle in any public place save in accordance with the conditions of a permit issued or countersigned by a Regional or State Transport Authority.

sections.title: Limits of speed
sections.content: No person shall drive a motor vehicle or cause or allow a motor vehicle to be driven in any public place at a speed exceeding the maximum speed or below the minimum speed fixed for the vehicle by or under this Act.

sections.title: Limits of weight and limitations on use
sections.content: The State Government may prescribe the conditions for issue of permits for transport vehicles by the State or Regional Transport Authorities and the maximum laden weight or maximum axle weight of any motor vehicle on any specified road.

sections.title: Duty to obey traffic signs
sections.content: Every driver of a motor vehicle shall drive the vehicle in conformity with any indication given by traffic signs and shall comply with all directions given by any police officer for the time being engaged in the regulation of traffic.

sections.title: Leaving vehicle in dangerous position
sections.content: No person in charge of a motor vehicle shall cause or allow the vehicle to remain stationary in such a position or in such condition or in such circumstances as to cause or be likely to cause danger, obstruction or undue inconvenience to other users of the road or to the passengers.

sections.title: Wearing of protective headgear
sections.content: Every person driving or riding on a motor cycle of any class shall, while in a public place, wear protective headgear conforming to prescribed standards, subject to exemptions for persons of the Sikh religion wearing a turban.

sections.title: Duty to produce licence and certificate of registration
sections.content: The driver of a motor vehicle in any public place shall, on demand by any police officer in uniform, produce his driving licence and the certificate of registration for examination. Where the driver is unable to produce these documents at the time of demand, he shall produce them at such place and within such period as the officer may specify.

sections.title: Duty of driver in case of accident and injury to a person
sections.content: When any person is injured or any property of a third party is damaged as a result of an accident involving a motor vehicle, the driver of the vehicle or other person in charge shall take all reasonable steps to secure medical attention for the injured person, give information about the accident to the nearest police station within twenty-four hours, and give the prescribed information to the insurer.

sections.title: Power of State Government to make rules
sections.content: The State Government may make rules for the purpose of carrying into effect the provisions of this Chapter, including rules governing licensing, registration, fitness, permits and traffic regulation within the State.

sections.title: Liability to pay compensation in certain cases on the principle of no fault
sections.content: Where the death or permanent disablement of any person has resulted from an accident arising out of the use of a motor vehicle, the owner of the vehicle shall be liable to pay compensation in respect of such death or disablement in accordance with the provisions of this section. The claimant shall not be required to plead and establish that the death or disablement was due to any wrongful act or neglect.

sections.title: Necessity for insurance against third-party risks
sections.content: No person shall use, except as a passenger, or cause or allow any other person to use, a motor vehicle in a public place, unless there is in force in relation to the use of the vehicle by that person or other person, as the case may be, a policy of insurance complying with the requirements of this Chapter.

sections.title: Requirements of policies and limits of liability
sections.content: Every policy of insurance under this Chapter shall be a policy issued by an authorised insurer, insure the person or persons specified in the policy against any liability incurred in respect of the death of or bodily injury to any person, including owners of goods or his authorised representative carried in the vehicle, caused by or arising out of the use of the vehicle in a public place.

sections.title: Duty of insurer to satisfy judgments against persons insured in respect of third party risks
sections.content: If, after a certificate of insurance has been issued under section 147(3), judgment in respect of any liability covered by the policy is obtained against any person insured by the policy, then the insurer shall, notwithstanding that the insurer may be entitled to avoid or cancel the policy, pay to the person entitled to the benefit of the decree any sum payable in respect of the liability.
citations: United India Insurance Co. Ltd. v. Lehru, (2003) 3 SCC 338

sections.title: Production of certain certificates, licence and permit in certain cases
sections.content: Any person driving a motor vehicle in any public place shall, on demand by any police officer in uniform, produce his driving licence, the certificate of insurance, the certificate of registration, the certificate of fitness if applicable, and the permit if applicable.

sections.title: Special provisions as to compensation in case of hit and run motor accident
sections.content: Notwithstanding anything contained in this Act, where death or grievous hurt to any person results from a hit and run motor accident, payment in accordance with the prescribed scheme shall be made by the Central Government out of the Solatium Fund.

sections.title: Special provisions as to payment of compensation on structured formula basis
sections.content: The owner of the motor vehicle or the authorised insurer shall be liable to pay compensation in the case of death or permanent disablement due to an accident arising out of the use of a motor vehicle, in accordance with the Second Schedule to the Act. The claimant shall not be required to plead or establish negligence on the part of the owner or driver.

sections.title: Application for compensation
sections.content: An application for compensation arising out of an accident involving the death of, or bodily injury to, persons, or damage to property, may be made by the person injured, the owner of the property, the legal representatives of the deceased, or by an agent duly authorised, to the Claims Tribunal having jurisdiction where the accident occurred or where the claimant resides.
citations: Sarla Verma v. Delhi Transport Corporation, (2009) 6 SCC 121
National Insurance Co. Ltd. v. Pranay Sethi, (2017) 16 SCC 680

sections.title: Award of the Claims Tribunal
sections.content: On receipt of an application for compensation, the Claims Tribunal shall, after giving notice of the application to the insurer and after giving the parties an opportunity of being heard, hold an inquiry into the claim and may make an award determining the amount of compensation which appears to it to be just, specifying the person or persons to whom compensation shall be paid.

sections.title: Appeals
sections.content: Subject to the provisions of sub-section (2), any person aggrieved by an award of a Claims Tribunal may, within ninety days from the date of the award, prefer an appeal to the High Court. No appeal shall lie against any award of a Claims Tribunal if the amount in dispute in the appeal is less than ten thousand rupees.

sections.title: General provision for punishment of offences
sections.content: Whoever contravenes any provision of this Act or of any rule, regulation or notification made thereunder shall, if no other penalty is provided for the offence, be punishable for the first offence with a fine of five hundred rupees, and for any second or subsequent offence with a fine of one thousand five hundred rupees.

sections.title: Driving dangerously
sections.content: Whoever drives a motor vehicle in any public place in a manner dangerous to the public, or which causes a sense of alarm or distress to occupants of the vehicle or to other users of the road, shall be punishable for the first offence with imprisonment up to one year or with fine up to five thousand rupees, or both, and for any subsequent offence committed within three years, with imprisonment up to two years or with fine up to ten thousand rupees, or both.

sections.title: Driving by a drunken person or under influence of drugs
sections.content: Whoever, while driving a motor vehicle, has alcohol exceeding 30 mg per 100 ml of blood detected in a test by a breath analyser, or is under the influence of a drug to such an extent as to be incapable of exercising proper control, shall be punishable for the first offence with imprisonment up to six months or with fine of ten thousand rupees, or both, and for a subsequent offence within three years, with imprisonment up to two years or with fine of fifteen thousand rupees, or both.

sections.title: Racing and trials of speed
sections.content: Whoever without the written consent of the State Government permits or takes part in a race or trial of speed of any kind between motor vehicles in any public place shall be punishable with imprisonment up to three months or with fine up to five hundred rupees, or both.

sections.title: Using vehicle without registration
sections.content: Whoever drives a motor vehicle or causes or allows a motor vehicle to be used in contravention of the provisions of section 39 shall be punishable for the first offence with a fine of two thousand to five thousand rupees, and for a subsequent offence with imprisonment up to one year or with fine of five thousand to ten thousand rupees, or both.

sections.title: Driving vehicle exceeding permissible weight
sections.content: Whoever drives a motor vehicle or causes or allows the vehicle to be driven in contravention of the provisions of section 113 shall be punishable with a fine of twenty thousand rupees, together with an additional amount of two thousand rupees per tonne of excess load, and for unloading of such excess load.

sections.title: Penalty for not wearing protective headgear
sections.content: Whoever drives or rides a motor cycle without wearing protective headgear in contravention of section 129 shall be punishable with a fine of one thousand rupees and disqualification for holding a licence for three months.

sections.title: Driving uninsured vehicle
sections.content: Whoever drives a motor vehicle or causes or allows a motor vehicle to be driven in contravention of section 146 shall be punishable for the first offence with imprisonment up to three months or with fine of two thousand rupees, or both, and for a subsequent offence with imprisonment up to three months or with fine of four thousand rupees, or both.

sections.title: Offences by juveniles
sections.content: Where an offence under this Act has been committed by a juvenile, the guardian of such juvenile or the owner of the motor vehicle shall be deemed to be guilty of the contravention and shall be liable to be proceeded against and punished accordingly, and the juvenile shall be punishable with imprisonment up to three years.
